items.title: Profile before trading
items.avoid: Picking a tradeoff from a textbook without measuring.
items.prefer: Profile the hot path; pick the move that buys the *measured* scarce resource.
items.why: Spending complexity on the wrong axis leaves the bottleneck untouched.

items.title: Bound every cache
items.prefer: Cap size by entries or bytes; set TTL where freshness matters.
items.why: Unbounded caches grow until they OOM; bounded caches are predictable.

items.title: Use streaming for anything bigger than memory
items.avoid: Reading the whole file / dataset / response into a list first.
items.prefer: Generators, `for line in file:`, chunked I/O.
items.why: Streaming flattens the memory curve and naturally back-pressures.
items.example:
```python
for chunk in stream_chunks(path, size=1<<20):
    process(chunk)
```

items.title: Drop temporary state when no longer needed
items.avoid: Keeping intermediate DP tables, embeddings, or batches in scope after the result is computed.
items.prefer: Reuse buffers; `del large_obj` and force GC if needed.
items.why: Long-lived references prevent collection; memory grows monotonically over a request.
items.example:
```python
result = compute(embeds)
del embeds   # let it be collected before the next batch
```

items.title: Make the tradeoff explicit in code and docs
items.avoid: Implicit caches and silent memoizations that confuse readers.
items.prefer: Comment the tradeoff (`# pay 200 MB index for O(log n) queries`) and tag the metric you're optimizing.
items.why: Future maintainers (and you in six months) need to know which axis was being optimized.
items.example:
```python
# Tradeoff: ~1.2 GB extra memory for 30× faster recall queries.
index = hnsw.build(embeddings, ef=200)
```
